description: Add the dev cluster Pulumi stack configuration and parameterize the linode-k3s provider for multi-environment support (environment tags and firewall IP allowlists).

userStories.title: Add cluster Pulumi dev stack configuration
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want a Pulumi.dev.yaml stack configuration for the cluster project so that I can provision a dev k3s cluster on Linode.

Create monorepo/infra/cluster/Pulumi.dev.yaml with:
- clusterType: linode-k3s
- clusterName: tdp-dev
- Linode token and root password as Pulumi secrets (placeholder secure values)
- Use a separate Linode API token from production (scoped permissions)

No code changes to the cluster layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Linode token is configured as a secure value placeholder
Root password is configured as a secure value placeholder
userStories.notes: Effort: S. No code changes needed — config file only.

userStories.title: Add environment tags to linode-k3s provider
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the linode-k3s provider to include environment-specific tags on the Linode instance so that instances are easily identifiable by environment.

Update monorepo/infra/cluster/providers/linode-k3s.ts:
- Add environment-specific tag from config (e.g., dev or production) to the Linode instance tags array (currently hardcoded ["k3s", "tdp"])
- Change the default cluster name from 'tdp-production' to a neutral default or require it via config (it is always overridden, but the default is misleading)
acceptanceCriteria: Linode instance tags include the environment name from config
Default cluster name is no longer hardcoded to tdp-production
Existing production stack config still produces correct tags

userStories.title: Parameterize firewall rules in linode-k3s provider
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the linode-k3s provider firewall rules to support IP allowlists so that SSH and K8s API access can be restricted to known IPs.

Update monorepo/infra/cluster/providers/linode-k3s.ts:
- Add a firewallAllowedIPs config key (defaults to ["0.0.0.0/0"])
- Apply the allowlist to SSH (22) and K8s API (6443) inbound rules
- HTTP (80) and HTTPS (443) remain open to all
- Resolves existing TODO comments on lines 35 and 62
- Both dev and production stacks should set this to known developer/CI IPs
acceptanceCriteria: firewallAllowedIPs config key is read from Pulumi config
SSH (port 22) inbound rules use the configured IP allowlist
K8s API (port 6443) inbound rules use the configured IP allowlist
HTTP (80) and HTTPS (443) remain open to 0.0.0.0/0
Default value is ["0.0.0.0/0"] when config key is unset
TODO comments about restricting IPs are resolved
